name: angler_priority_v1
description: Pragmatic deployment priors for fish species that anglers are more likely to photograph in the app. These are not training labels and should be revised with region-specific app telemetry, licensing, creel, and catch-report data.

north-america.description: Initial North America MVP prior over the current app taxonomy.
north-america.method: Mapped broad U.S. recreational target groups onto available MVP labels. Black bass, panfish, catfish/bullheads, trout, and crappie dominate non-Great-Lakes freshwater effort; red drum and snapper are retained for saltwater MVP coverage.

sources.title: 2016 National Survey of Fishing, Hunting, and Wildlife-Associated Recreation
sources.note: Official U.S. survey used for freshwater, Great Lakes, and saltwater target-species priorities.

europe.description: Initial Europe prior over the current Europe taxonomy.
europe.method: Blends Europe-wide inland fishery references, country/coarse-fishing target patterns, and current taxonomy coverage. Freshwater angling is weighted higher because the current app dataset and user workflow are catch-photo oriented; marine/coastal species remain represented for future expansion.

sources.title: FAO Inland fisheries of Europe
sources.note: Lists important European inland fish for commercial, food, and recreational use.

sources.title: FAO Inland fisheries of Europe questionnaire summary
sources.note: Includes angler catch/preference signals for roach, pike, bream, perch, carp, eel, tench, catfish, pike-perch, chub, and trout.

sources.title: UK common coarse species guide
sources.note: Practical coarse-angling source for common UK species patterns.
